patch.value:
## Gap

`ExtensionApi` has 14 `register*` methods; profiles are the only authoring surface in the SDK barrel (`defineProjectProfile`) with no `register*` counterpart. `pm profile list/show/apply` reads only `BUILTIN_PROFILES` (agile/ops/research). `packages/pm-kanban` exports `kanbanProfile` but it can never reach `pm profile`.

## Design

- **Capability**: reuse the existing `schema` capability (a profile is fundamentally a schema+config bundle; an extension registering a profile already needs `schema` to apply its types/fields). No capability-contract version bump.
- **Policy surface**: add `schema.profiles` to `KNOWN_EXTENSION_POLICY_SURFACES`, alongside `schema.itemtypes`/`schema.migrations`.
- **Registry**: new `RegisteredExtensionProjectProfile { layer, name, profile }`; `ExtensionRegistrationRegistry.profiles`; `ExtensionRegistrationCounts.profiles`.
- **Loader**: `api.registerProfile(profile)` validates the definition, gates on `schema` + `schema.profiles`, pushes to `registrations.profiles`.
- **Resolution**: `pm profile list/show/apply` merges `getActiveExtensionRegistrations()?.profiles` with `BUILTIN_PROFILES`; builtin names are reserved (extension collision warns + is ignored); each profile carries a `source` (builtin | package name) surfaced in CLI/MCP output.
- **SDK**: `assertRegisteredProfile` testing helper (declarative surface → assert only, mirroring item types/fields); `composeExtension` blueprint `profiles` auto-wires `registerProfile`; `deriveExtensionCapabilities` maps profiles → `schema`; `describeExtensionActivation`/`describeExtensionBlueprint`/markdown include profiles.
- **Dogfood**: `packages/pm-kanban` calls `api.registerProfile(kanbanProfile)` so `pm profile apply kanban` works once installed — the live exemplar.
- **Docs**: SDK.md / EXTENSIONS.md document the surface.

## Acceptance

- `pm profile list` shows extension-registered profiles with their source.
- `pm profile apply <extension-profile>` stages it idempotently like a builtin.
- Full `define→register→assert→describe→derive→compose` parity for profiles.
- 100% test + docstring coverage; full.json + contracts regenerated; bots green.

Close the one remaining gap in the extension authoring tripod: the ExtensionApi exposes 14 register* surfaces (commands, flags, item types/fields, migrations, renderers, parser/preflight/service overrides, importers/exporters, search providers, vector store adapters) but NO way to contribute a PROJECT PROFILE. A profile is the single most powerful customization primitive — it bundles item types + custom statuses + fields + per-type workflows + config knobs + create templates + package recommendations into one declarative archetype that `pm profile apply` stages idempotently. Today `pm profile list/show/apply` resolves ONLY from the 3 core-baked BUILTIN_PROFILES (agile/ops/research). The SDK already ships defineProjectProfile (authoring identity) and packages/pm-kanban exports a kanbanProfile ProjectProfileDefinition, but it is UNREACHABLE: there is no `pm profile apply kanban` path because nothing registers it. This makes the 'any project archetype models its domain natively' promise of epic pm-8jdc incomplete for third-party packages.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented on branch feat-extension-registered-project-profiles. Core: api.registerProfile + RegisteredExtensionProjectProfile + registry/counts + loader gate (schema cap + schema.profiles policy surface) + validation/normalization. Resolution: resolveProfileCatalog/resolveProfileEntry merge builtins + extension profiles with source labels; builtin names reserved (collision warns+ignored). SDK: assertRegisteredProfile + harness assertProfile; composeExtension blueprint.profiles autowire; deriveExtensionCapabilities profiles->schema; describeExtension(Activation|Blueprint)+markdown include profiles. Dogfood: pm-kanban activate() registers kanbanProfile. Docs: SDK.md/EXTENSIONS.md/COMMANDS.md/pm-kanban README. Manual e2e (temp tracker): installed kanban -> `pm profile list` shows kanban [builtin-kanban-profile]; show/apply(dry-run/real/idempotent) all work; --json carries source/package/warnings; --no-extensions falls back to builtins; a crafted rogue 'agile' profile is ignored with a warning (builtin wins). Gates: typecheck/eslint/jscpd/static-docstring all green; contracts full.json regenerated (schema.profiles surface).
